title: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet

regions.label: Controlled method record

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Validation packet control and method scope

regions.goldSection: Page 1 - Validation packet control and method scope

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Field, Controlled value.
The row order is preserved as: Study -> Instrument -> Column -> Injection -> Study interval -> Reporting basis.
The controlled study identifier is MV-PFAS-24-017.
The instrument is a SCIEX 6500+ QTRAP operated in ESI negative mode.
Results are reported in ng/L in the original sample, with U95 where quantified.

regions.label: Source precedence diagram

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Validation packet control and method scope

regions.goldSection: Page 1 - Validation packet control and method scope

leaves.expectation: The precedence order is signed QA correction, then accepted integration, then provisional LIMS export.
A signed QA correction controls only the fields it amends.

regions.label: Bottle manifest

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Validation packet control and method scope

regions.goldSection: Page 1 - Validation packet control and method scope

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Sample ID, Matrix, Collected, Received, Preserved, Release path.
DW-240408-03 follows the MS/MSD release path.
DW-240409-04 follows the corrected release path.

regions.label: Calibration and recovery equations

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration model, transitions, and limits

regions.goldSection: Page 2 - Calibration model, transitions, and limits

leaves.expectation: Response ratio is written as R = A_native / A_IS.
Concentration is written as C_sample = ((R - b) / m) x DF.
Spike recovery is written as Recovery % = ((C_spiked - C_native) / C_added) x 100.
Expanded uncertainty is written as U95 = 2 x sqrt(u_cal^2 + u_rep^2 + u_vol^2 + u_rec^2).

regions.label: Nested transition and limit table

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration model, transitions, and limits

regions.goldSection: Page 2 - Calibration model, transitions, and limits

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Analyte, Internal standard, Quantifier, Qualifier, RT min, Range ng/L, LOD / LOQ ng/L.
The row order is preserved as: PFBA -> PFPeA -> PFHxA -> PFHpA -> PFOA -> PFNA -> PFDA -> PFBS -> PFHxS -> PFOS -> 6:2 FTS -> HFPO-DA.
For PFOA, Quantifier is 413>369.
For PFOA, Qualifier is 413>169.
For PFOA, Internal standard is 13C8-PFOA.
For PFOA, RT min is 4.41.
For PFOA, Range ng/L is 0.25-80.
For PFOA, LOD / LOQ ng/L is 0.08 / 0.25.
For PFOS, Internal standard is 13C8-PFOS.
For PFOS, RT min is 5.64.
For PFOS, Range ng/L is 0.50-100.
For PFOS, LOD / LOQ ng/L is 0.22 / 0.50.
For HFPO-DA, Internal standard is 13C3-HFPO-DA.
For HFPO-DA, Quantifier is 329>285.
For HFPO-DA, Qualifier is 329>169.
For HFPO-DA, RT min is 3.71.
For 6:2 FTS, LOD / LOQ ng/L is 0.31 / 1.00.

regions.label: Qualifier contract

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration model, transitions, and limits

regions.goldSection: Page 2 - Calibration model, transitions, and limits

leaves.expectation: U means not detected at the LOQ; preserve the leading < sign and the LOQ.
J means estimated concentration; preserve both the numeric value and J.
A dash means no qualifier and must not be expanded into an invented qualifier.

regions.label: Calibration curve points

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration curves and residual disposition

regions.goldSection: Page 3 - Calibration curves and residual disposition

leaves.expectation: PFOA has response ratio 0.021 at 0.25 ng/L.
PFOA has response ratio 0.077 at 1 ng/L.
PFOA has response ratio 0.406 at 5 ng/L.
PFOA has response ratio 1.393 at 20 ng/L.
PFOA has response ratio 5.986 at 80 ng/L.
PFOS has response ratio 0.042 at 0.5 ng/L.
PFOS has response ratio 0.184 at 2 ng/L.
PFOS has response ratio 0.958 at 10 ng/L.
PFOS has response ratio 4.753 at 50 ng/L.
PFOS has response ratio 8.177 at 100 ng/L.
HFPO-DA has response ratio 0.044 at 0.5 ng/L.
HFPO-DA has response ratio 0.175 at 2 ng/L.
HFPO-DA has response ratio 0.790 at 10 ng/L.
HFPO-DA has response ratio 4.400 at 50 ng/L.
HFPO-DA has response ratio 6.632 at 80 ng/L.

regions.label: Calibration chart axes, legend, and trend

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration curves and residual disposition

regions.goldSection: Page 3 - Calibration curves and residual disposition

leaves.expectation: The chart uses log concentration in ng/L on the horizontal x-axis and response ratio on the vertical y-axis.
The legend maps the blue line and points to the PFOA calibration series.
The legend maps the green line and points to the PFOS calibration series.
The legend maps the amber line and points to the HFPO-DA calibration series.
Each panel uses weighted 1/x^2 calibration and labels every point with both concentration and response.
The PFOA series rises monotonically from response ratio 0.021 at its lowest standard to 5.986 at its highest standard.
The PFOS series rises monotonically from response ratio 0.042 at its lowest standard to 8.177 at its highest standard.
The HFPO-DA series rises monotonically from response ratio 0.044 at its lowest standard to 6.632 at its highest standard.

regions.label: Residual disposition

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Calibration curves and residual disposition

regions.goldSection: Page 3 - Calibration curves and residual disposition

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Analyte, Weighted R2, Max abs back-calc residual, Disposition.
The row order is preserved as: PFOA -> PFOS -> HFPO-DA.
PFOS weighted R2 is 0.9978 and its 14.6% maximum residual occurs at 100 ng/L; the upper point is retained.
HFPO-DA weighted R2 is 0.9989 with a 9.7% residual at 0.50 ng/L and no curvature flag.
Weighted R2 must be >=0.995; standards must be within +/-15%, except the lowest level may be within +/-20%.

regions.label: Table V4 rows PFBA through PFNA

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Table V4 - Accuracy and precision

regions.goldSection: Page 4 - Table V4 accuracy and precision, part 1

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Analyte, Low rec / RSD %, Mid rec / RSD %, High rec / RSD %, n, State.
The row order is preserved as: PFBA -> PFPeA -> PFHxA -> PFHpA -> PFOA -> PFNA.
PFOA low/mid/high recovery and RSD pairs are 98.0/6.8, 103.0/3.7, and 101.0/3.2%, with n=7 and state Pass.
PFHxA low recovery/RSD is 91.0/10.3%, with n=7 and state Pass.

regions.label: Validation study design

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Table V4 - Accuracy and precision

regions.goldSection: Page 4 - Table V4 accuracy and precision, part 1

leaves.expectation: The low level is LOQ or 2 x LOQ, with 7 replicates on 7 days.
All levels require 70-130% recovery and RSD <=20%.
Table V4 continues on page 5 with PFDA through HFPO-DA under repeated headers.

regions.label: Continued Table V4 rows PFDA through HFPO-DA

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Table V4 - Accuracy and precision (continued)

regions.goldSection: Page 5 - Table V4 accuracy and precision, part 2

leaves.expectation: The row order is preserved as: PFDA -> PFBS -> PFHxS -> PFOS -> 6:2 FTS -> HFPO-DA.
PFOS low/mid/high recovery and RSD pairs are 89.0/12.6, 95.0/6.7, and 96.0/5.1%, with n=7 and state Pass.
6:2 FTS low/mid/high recovery and RSD pairs are 86.0/14.8, 92.0/7.4, and 94.0/6.3%, with n=7 and state Pass.
This is the continuation of page 4 Table V4; repeated headers are not data rows.

regions.label: Validation exception register

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Table V4 - Accuracy and precision (continued)

regions.goldSection: Page 5 - Table V4 accuracy and precision, part 2

leaves.expectation: EX-24-011 is the omitted-fortification 6:2 FTS low-level day-2 result at 61% recovery.
EX-24-011 is excluded from n=7 and replaced by a valid replicate included in the 86.0% mean.
EX-24-014 retains the PFOS low-level day-6 result because 89% recovery and 12.6% RSD are within criteria.

regions.label: Scanned preparation ledger

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Extraction, fortification, and dilution bench record

regions.goldSection: Page 6 - Scanned extraction, fortification, and dilution record

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Prep ID, Material / sample, Initial, Aliquot, Final, Recorded target / DF.
The row order is preserved as: SPK-01 -> SPK-02 -> DIL-03 -> DIL-04 -> DIL-05.
SPK-01 uses 1.000 ng/uL stock, 10.00 uL aliquot, and 1.000 L final volume for a 10.00 ng/L LCS target.
For DIL-03, Final is 5.000 mL.
For DIL-03, Recorded target / DF is DF 10.00.
For DIL-05, Final is 0.800 mL.
For DIL-05, Recorded target / DF is DF 2.00.

regions.label: Scanned extraction ledger and signatures

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Extraction, fortification, and dilution bench record

regions.goldSection: Page 6 - Scanned extraction, fortification, and dilution record

leaves.expectation: DW-240408-03 uses 249.0 mL bottle volume, 1.000 mL extract, SPE lot SPE-24-118, and 50.0 uL surrogate.
SPK-01 and SPK-02 are verified against CRM certificate PFAS-MIX-2319 expiring 30 Sep 2024.
DIL-05 was prepared after MX-24-041; the DIL-04 vial remains in audit storage.
M. Iyer signed at 08:42 and L. Chen witnessed at 08:51 on 10 Apr 2024.

regions.label: Scanned instrument sequence

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
LC-MS/MS sequence and technical review

regions.goldSection: Page 7 - Scanned LC-MS/MS sequence and technical review

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Seq, Type, Vial, Sample / control, Start, IS rec, Carryover, State.
Sequence 17 is CCV-10 at 11:38 with 95% IS recovery and Accepted state.
Sequence 18 is DW-240409-04 at 11:51 with 38% IS recovery and state HOLD MX-24-041.
Sequence 19 is DW-240409-04-RI at 12:18 with 91% IS recovery and Accepted state.
Sequence 21 is closing CCV-10-CLOSE at 12:44 with 93% IS recovery and Accepted state.

regions.label: Scanned sequence review annotations

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
LC-MS/MS sequence and technical review

regions.goldSection: Page 7 - Scanned LC-MS/MS sequence and technical review

leaves.expectation: MX-24-041 opened at 11:58 because sequence 18 IS recovery was below 50%.
The authorization permits one reinjection after a blank and needle-seat flush.
If reinjection IS is 50-150%, the original remains only in the audit trail.
M. Iyer signed 19 Apr 2024 14:05; L. Chen signed technical review 22 Apr 2024 09:40.

regions.label: Instrument chromatogram and integration plate

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Raw integration and chromatogram review

regions.goldSection: Page 8 - Raw integration and chromatogram review

leaves.expectation: Panel A is the LRB-240410-01 PFOS quantifier trace.
Panel A marks 5.64 min, a 38-44 cps noise window, no integrated peak, and file 240410_10.wiff.
Panel B is the DW-240408-03 PFOA quantifier trace.
Panel B shows 4.41 min, area 25,621, quant/qual ratio 0.31, and file 240410_14.wiff.
Panel C is the DW-240409-04 13C8-PFOA original trace linked to sequence 18 and IR-18A.
Panel C shows original internal-standard recovery of 38% at 4.39 min.
Panel D is the DW-240409-04 13C8-PFOA reinjection trace linked to sequence 19 and IR-19B.
Panel D shows reinjection internal-standard recovery of 91% at 4.40 min.

regions.label: Panel routing register

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Raw integration and chromatogram review

regions.goldSection: Page 8 - Raw integration and chromatogram review

leaves.expectation: Panel C routes to original integration IR-18A and must not be treated as an accepted result.
Panel D routes to reinjection integration IR-19B and does not by itself define correction scope.
Exact RT, area, ratio, and IS-recovery values come from the instrument annotations; the routing register supplies panel identity only.

regions.label: QC measured values

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Blank, recovery, and continuing-calibration review

regions.goldSection: Page 9 - Blank, recovery, and continuing-calibration review

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Control, Analyte, Measured, Review criterion, Review state.
The source row order preserves each Control/Analyte pair from the two LRB rows through the CCV-10-CLOSE row for PFOA.
LRB PFOA is <0.25 ng/L U and PFOS is <0.50 ng/L U; both meet their blank criteria.
LCS measured concentrations are 9.62 ng/L PFOA and 9.14 ng/L PFOS.
DW-240408-03-MS PFOA is 13.02 ng/L; native PFOA is 3.42 ng/L.
DW-240408-03-MSD PFOA is 13.22 ng/L and 6:2 FTS is 8.40 ng/L with native <1.00 U.
Opening CCV-10 PFOA is 9.48 ng/L and closing CCV-10-CLOSE PFOA is 10.31 ng/L; both meet 85-115% of 10.00.

regions.label: Cross-page QC calculations

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Extraction, fortification, and dilution bench record
PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Blank, recovery, and continuing-calibration review

regions.goldSection: Page 9 - Blank, recovery, and continuing-calibration review

leaves.expectation: Using the 10.00 ng/L SPK-01 target, LCS recoveries are 96.2% for PFOA and 91.4% for PFOS.
Using the 10.00 ng/L SPK-02 target and the 3.42 ng/L PFOA native result, the PFOA MS and MSD recoveries are 96.0% and 98.0%.
The PFOA MS/MSD recovery RPD is 2.1%.
Using the 10.00 ng/L SPK-02 target and treating the 6:2 FTS native result marked U as zero, MSD recovery is 84.0%.

regions.label: Scanned maintenance incident chronology

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Instrument maintenance and reinjection authorization

regions.goldSection: Page 10 - Scanned maintenance and reinjection authorization

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Time, Observation / action, Measured check, Initials.
Sequence 18 was flagged at 11:55 for 13C8-PFOA recovery of 38%.
Needle-seat inspection found visible salt film at 12:04.
The needle seat was flushed three times with 500 uL of 50:50 methanol:water.
The system blank had PFOS carryover <0.08 ng/L and the IS check recovered 96%.

regions.label: Scanned reinjection authorization

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Instrument maintenance and reinjection authorization

regions.goldSection: Page 10 - Scanned maintenance and reinjection authorization

leaves.expectation: Only DW-240409-04 may be reinjected, exactly once, using retained vial DIL-05.
Acceptance requires IS recovery of 50-150% and blank carryover below LOQ.
If the gate passes, both integrations go to QA; this maintenance record does not select a final concentration.
Sequence 18, IR-18A, and original vial DIL-04 must remain in the audit trail.
M. Iyer signed at 12:22 and L. Chen authorized at 12:24 on 19 Apr 2024.

regions.label: Provisional LIMS export

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
LIMS export and signed QA correction

regions.goldSection: Page 11 - LIMS export and signed QA correction

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Export row, Sample, Analyte, Result, Qual., Source, Status.
The row order is preserved as: E-104 -> E-105 -> E-106.
Provisional row E-104 reports DW-240409-04 PFOA as 1.84 ng/L J from sequence 18/IR-18A.
Provisional row E-105 reports DW-240409-04 PFOS as <0.50 ng/L U from sequence 18/IR-18A.
Ready row E-106 reports DW-240409-05 PFOA as 4.87 ng/L with no qualifier from sequence 20/IR-20A.

regions.label: Signed QA correction CR-24-044

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
LIMS export and signed QA correction

regions.goldSection: Page 11 - LIMS export and signed QA correction

leaves.expectation: CR-24-044 applies only to DW-240409-04; other batch results are unchanged.
CR-24-044 supersedes sequence 18/IR-18A fields and makes accepted sequence 19/IR-19B controlling.
DW-240409-04 PFOA changes from 1.84 ng/L J to 1.06 ng/L J.
DW-240409-04 PFOS remains <0.50 ng/L U with no qualifier change.
The correction reason is sequence 18 internal-standard recovery below the 50-150% criterion.
The correction must not overwrite the audit trail.
L. Chen signed 22 Apr 2024 09:40 and R. Patel signed 22 Apr 2024 16:10.

regions.label: Preliminary-to-correction reading order

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
LIMS export and signed QA correction

regions.goldSection: Page 11 - LIMS export and signed QA correction

leaves.expectation: Preserve the controlled record order: provisional PRELIM-LIMS-0419 first, signed CR-24-044 second.

regions.label: Released result table

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Final signed validation and batch determination

regions.goldSection: Page 12 - Final signed validation and batch determination

leaves.expectation: The field schema is preserved in order: Result ID, Sample, Analyte, Released result, Qual., U95, Authority.
The row order is preserved as: R-01 -> R-02 -> R-03 -> R-04 -> R-05 -> R-06 -> R-07 -> R-08.
For R-02, Released result is 2.18 ng/L.
For R-02, Qual. is -.
For R-02, U95 is 0.42 ng/L.
R-03 releases DW-240408-02 PFOS at 0.62 ng/L J with U95 0.18 ng/L.
For R-04, Released result is 3.42 ng/L.
For R-04, Qual. is -.
For R-04, U95 is 0.55 ng/L.
R-06 releases DW-240409-04 PFOA at 1.06 ng/L J with U95 0.25 ng/L under CR-24-044.
R-07 releases DW-240409-04 PFOS as <0.50 ng/L U under CR-24-044.
For R-08, Released result is 4.87 ng/L.
For R-08, Qual. is -.
For R-08, U95 is 0.72 ng/L.

regions.label: Final determination and signatures

sectionPath: PFAS Method Validation and Batch Release Packet
Final signed validation and batch determination

regions.goldSection: Page 12 - Final signed validation and batch determination

leaves.expectation: Calibration is accepted based on weighted fits and residual review.
Recovery and precision are accepted with valid n=7 and documented EX-24-011 replacement.
DW-240409-04 is accepted as corrected from sequence 19/IR-19B under CR-24-044.
The batch is RELEASED with J and U qualifiers retained exactly.
Release does not erase provisional exports, rejected integrations, or maintenance records.
L. Chen signed technical review at 09:40 and R. Patel released at 16:10 on 22 Apr 2024.

leaves.expectation: The controlling lineage is the sequence 18 low-IS flag, signed maintenance and reinjection authorization, accepted sequence 19 with IR-19B, signed CR-24-044, and final release.
